message.content:
You are mapping the PROGRESS / ATTEMPTS / MASTERY subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts
- server/src/models/RoadmapAttempt.model.ts
- server/src/models/Test.model.ts (the attempt-relevant parts)
- server/src/services/testResult.service.ts
- server/src/services/profileStats.service.ts
- server/src/services/trial.service.ts
Goal: explain how a student's progress, mastery, and unlocking of roadmap nodes work. Specifically:
- How is per-user progress stored? Per node? What states (locked/available/done/mastery score)?
- How does completing a test update progress and unlock the next node?
- Is mastery tracked at node level only, or at topic/subtopic/knowledge level?
- What is "trial" — a trial mode? How does it differ from the full roadmap flow?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. Quote progress schema and the unlock logic in evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll analyze the progress/attempts/mastery subsystem. Let me read all the specified files in parallel.

This is a focused subsystem-mapping task. The progress/unlock logic clearly lives partly in files I haven't read yet (`roadmap.service`, `roadmap.rules`, `roadmap.types`). Let me read those to complete the picture, plus find where attempts/progress are written.

input.command: find /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/roadmap -type f 2>/dev/null; echo "---TYPES---"; find /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/types -name '*roadmap*' -type f 2>/dev/null; echo "---SERVICES---"; ls /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services | grep -i roadmap
input.description: Locate roadmap rules, types, and service files
Find files that read/write progress and attempts

content.text: `roadmap.service.ts` is where the unlock logic and progress writes live. Let me read it, the rules, and the types.

I now have the complete picture of the unlock/mastery logic. Let me confirm where `recordTestSubmitted` and `applyTrialKtpResults` are invoked (the test-completion → progress write entry points) and how trial scoring rows are built.

input.description: Find callers of progress-write methods and trial mastery row builder

content.text: Let me read the test.controller.ts submit flow and the trial mastery row builder to complete the test-completion → progress wiring.
I now have the full picture. I have all evidence needed to map the subsystem.

entities.name: UserRoadmapProgress
fields: canonicalVersion: number — min 1 (version of canonical roadmap this snapshot was merged against)
nodes: IUserRoadmapNodeProgress[] — embedded subdoc array, default []
relationships: 1 doc per (userId, subjectId) — compound UNIQUE index {userId:1, subjectId:1} (line 36)
nodes embed per-node progress; nodeId is a string key matching ICanonicalRoadmapNode.nodeId (live-built from KTP, e.g. 'ktp:<id>')
NOT linked to RoadmapAttempt by ref — attempts are a separate audit/idempotency collection
entities.notes: Progress is stored as ONE document per user+subject with an EMBEDDED nodes array (not one row per node). There is no persisted 'locked/available' state — availability is computed at read time. States actually persisted per node: mastered(bool), bestScore(0..100), lowScoreFailCount(0..N). normalizeStoredNodeProgress (line 58) migrates legacy docs that used progressStatus/attemptsCount: if legacy progressStatus==='mastered' it backfills bestScore=max(legacy,70).

entities.name: IUserRoadmapNodeProgress (NodeProgressSchema, embedded)
fields: bestScore: number — required, default 0, min 0 max 100 (global best % over the node, NOT a rolling window)
lowScoreFailCount: number — default 0, min 0 (consecutive sub-80% knowledge-test fails)
lessons: IUserLessonProgress[] — default undefined (per-lesson read progress inside the node)
lessonReadAt: Date — set when the LAST lesson of the node is completed ('material done')
relationships: nodeId maps 1:1 to a canonical node (a KTP topic). The node IS the unit of mastery.
entities.notes: Mastery is tracked ONLY at node level (node = KTP topic). There is NO per-question or per-knowledge-component mastery. Two sub-structures exist: mastery/scoring (mastered/bestScore/lowScoreFailCount) and lesson-reading gate (lessons[]/lessonReadAt). Type def at roadmap.types.ts:101.

entities.name: IUserLessonProgress (LessonProgressSchema, embedded)
fields: readAt: Date — when this lesson was completed
relationships: lessonId validated against nodeLessonIds(subjectId,node) — the ordered lessons of the node (roadmap.service.ts:946)
entities.notes: Drives sequential lesson gating WITHIN a node (must complete previous lessons before next). Independent of test mastery.

entities.name: RoadmapAttempt
relationships: UNIQUE compound index {userId:1, sessionId:1} (line 18) — this is the IDEMPOTENCY key for recordTestSubmitted
One row per submitted knowledge-map test attempt; references node by string nodeId, subject by ref
entities.notes: This is an append-only attempt log used purely for idempotency/audit. It does NOT itself store mastery; recordTestSubmitted writes the attempt AND separately recomputes the embedded node progress. A duplicate (userId,sessionId) short-circuits to idempotent return.

entities.name: Test (attempt-relevant parts)
fields: questions: IQuestion[] — must be exactly 10 valid ENT questions (validator line 109)
relationships: Each IQuestion.relatedContent embeds {chapterId, topicId, pages, topicTitle} (RelatedContentSchema line 5) — topicId is what links a question back to a book topic, then fanned out to KTP nodes
Test is NOT linked to a roadmap node directly; the link is established at submit time via roadmapNodeId in the request body
entities.notes: Tests are content/cache documents (findCachedTest static line 124). The attempt-relevant signal is q.relatedContent.topicId/chapterId, consumed by testResult.service (topicsToReview) and trialTopicMastery.util (KTP fan-out). Each test is fixed at 10 questions.

entities.name: ITestHistory (embedded in User, written on submit)
fields: aiFeedback: lazily generated, NOT on submit
relationships: Consumed by testResult.service.buildResultSummary/buildBreakdown and by roadmap AI insights (getPersonalSnapshot reads userDoc.testHistory)
entities.notes: This is the per-test history; distinct from RoadmapAttempt. Roadmap progress is updated only when roadmapNodeId+roadmapSessionId are present in the same submit.

flows.name: Per-node progress storage & availability (read path: getPersonalSnapshot)
steps: roadmap.service.ts:284 getPersonalSnapshot(userId, subjectId)
resolveCanonical (line 115) builds the canonical node list LIVE from KtpCatalog (KTP topics → nodes via buildKtpCanonicalNodes), falling back to a stored CanonicalRoadmap doc
Load UserRoadmapProgress doc; if missing, upsert a merged default snapshot (line 298-307); if node count or canonicalVersion drift, re-merge (line 309-316)
mergeProgressWithCanonical (line 174) reconciles stored nodes against canonical order, filling defaults via defaultProgress (line 40: mastered:false,bestScore:0,lowScoreFailCount:0)
masteredSet (line 48) = set of nodeIds with p.mastered===true
availabilityForNode (line 60): no prerequisites => 'available'; else 'available' iff EVERY prerequisite nodeId is in masteredSet, else 'locked'
Build per-node view: availability, mastered, bestScore, lowScoreFailCount, knowledgeMapTestBlocked = lowScoreFailCount >= 3 (line 343)
Compute frontier = available && !mastered nodes; rank by in-progress > unlocksCount > lowest bestScore (compareFrontier line 99); top-K become recommendations; first = nextRecommended
progressPercent = round(masteredCount / totalNodes * 100) (line 406)
flows.aiCalls: Optional AI enrichment ONLY if options.includeAiInsights && process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY (line 437). Calls roadmapAIService.enrichPersonalRoadmap with nodesSummary + last-20 testHistory; produces coachSummary, nextStepExplanation, per-node aiHint. Wrapped in try/catch, falls back to base on failure. Recommendation RANKING itself is pure heuristic (no AI).

flows.name: Completing a knowledge-map test → update mastery + unlock next node
steps: Client POST /tests/submit with testId, answers, roadmapNodeId, roadmapSessionId (test.controller.ts:302)
checkAnswers computes resultSummary.scorePercent; testHistory pushed to User (controller line 309-328)
If roadmapNodeId & roadmapSessionId present (line 333): roadmapService.recordTestSubmitted({userId,subjectId,nodeId,scorePercent,sessionId,submittedAt}) (line 335)
recordTestSubmitted (roadmap.service.ts:776) validates nodeId exists in canonical (line 790)
IDEMPOTENCY: if RoadmapAttempt {userId,sessionId} exists (line 793) OR insert hits dup-key 11000 (line 815), return idempotent snapshot, NO progress mutation
Else RoadmapAttempt.create(...) appends the attempt row (line 805)

Recompute node: cur = existing or defaultProgress; bestScore = max(cur.bestScore, scorePercent) (line 833); mastered = cur.mastered || isMasteredByBestScore(bestScore) i.e. bestScore>=70 (line 834); lowScoreFailCount = scorePercent>=80 ? 0 : min(3, prevFail+1) (line 836-839)
Write updated node back into nodes[] and persist via findOneAndUpdate upsert (line 853)

Call getPersonalSnapshot to recompute availability for ALL nodes — a node whose prerequisites just became mastered flips locked→available automatically (no explicit 'unlock node' write). Return updatedNodesDelta + nextRecommended + topRecommendations (line 865-875)
flows.aiCalls: None in the write path. (recordTestSubmitted calls getPersonalSnapshot WITHOUT includeAiInsights, so no LLM call.)

flows.name: Knowledge-test gating (3-strike block + 'Освоил' reset)
steps: Before starting a node test, assertKnowledgeMapTestAllowed (roadmap.service.ts:151) loads merged progress; if node.lowScoreFailCount >= ROADMAP_MAX_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_FAILS_BEFORE_BLOCK (3) it throws 403 (line 167)
Called from POST /tests/generate and POST /tests/solo/start when roadmapNodeId in body (test.controller.ts:266, 366)
Each sub-80% submit increments lowScoreFailCount (capped at 3); a >=80% submit resets it to 0 (recordTestSubmitted line 836)
User can also reset by reading material: acknowledgeMaterialMastery (line 997) sets lowScoreFailCount=0 (the 'Освоил' button)

flows.name: Lesson reading gate within a node
steps: markLessonComplete (roadmap.service.ts:933) validates lessonId via nodeLessonIds; enforces sequential gating — all earlier lessons by order must be completed (line 958-962)
Pushes {lessonId, readAt} into node.lessons; when ALL lessonIds completed, sets node.lessonReadAt (line 971-976)
markLessonRead (line 878) is a coarser variant that just stamps lessonReadAt
flows.aiCalls: Lesson CONTENT may be AI-consolidated (nodeLessonContent.service / INodeLessonContent cache), but the progress write itself makes no LLM call. Note: completing lessons does NOT set mastered — only test scores do.

flows.name: Trial (пробник) mode → mastery
steps: TrialService.buildPlan (trial.service.ts:86) constructs an ENT-shaped exam plan: 3 fixed main blocks (История Казахстана 20q, Мат.грамотность 10q, Грамотность чтения 10q — MAIN_TRIAL_TITLES/BLOCKS lines 4-17) + 2 chosen profile subjects (40q / 50pts each)
Each step is pinned to subject.books[0], first chapter (by order), first topic via assertSubjectNodeForTrial (line 19); useFullBook:true

On submit with forTrial:true (test.controller.ts:310), computeTrialTopicMasteryRows (trialTopicMastery.util.ts:14) tallies correctness per (chapterId,topicId) from q.relatedContent, fans each book-topic out to its KTP nodeIds (mapBookTopicToKtpNodeIds), keeps rows with score>=80 (max per node)
trial.controller.ts:35 calls roadmapService.applyTrialKtpResults(userId, rows)

applyTrialKtpResults (roadmap.service.ts:735): for each row with isMasteredByTrialScore (>=80, line 742), bestScore=max(cur,score); mastered = cur.mastered || trial>=80 || best>=70 (line 755); writes node progress (line 763). Does NOT touch lowScoreFailCount.
flows.aiCalls: None in mastery application. Question generation for the trial is the same AI test-generation pipeline as regular tests (out of scope here).

flows.name: Profile stats (topics studied) derived from progress
steps: profileStats.service.ts:57 computeTopicsStudied iterates getLearnerSubjectIds and calls roadmapService.getProgressCounts(userId, sid) (line 62)
getProgressCounts (roadmap.service.ts:525): nodesTotal = canonical node count; nodesMastered = stored nodes with mastered && nodeId in canonical (line 541-543) — lightweight, no upsert, no AI
dailyStreak computed from SoloAttempt ranked attempts (line 30); subjects selected from Subject counts + user.profileSubjectPairId (line 74)

input.reuse: Canonical roadmap is REGENERATED live on every read from KtpCatalog via resolveCanonical/buildKtpCanonicalNodes (roadmap.service.ts:115); the stored CanonicalRoadmap doc is only a fallback and is deleted on rebuild (line 1066). 'Version' is a derived timestamp (max(subject.updatedAt, ktp.updatedAt)/1000), not a stored counter. Per-user progress IS persisted and reused: one UserRoadmapProgress doc per (user,subject), reconciled against canonical via mergeProgressWithCanonical each read (defaults filled, drift re-saved when node count or canonicalVersion changes, lines 309-316). Availability/locked-state and recommendations are NOT cached — recomputed every snapshot. RoadmapAttempt provides idempotency caching keyed on UNIQUE {userId, sessionId} so a re-submitted session does not double-mutate mastery. Lesson content (INodeLessonContent) is AI-generated once and cached by sourceHash (regenerated when mapped source texts change). bestScore is a global max, NOT a rolling window (ROADMAP_BEST_SCORE_WINDOW_N is declared but unused — comment at roadmap.rules.ts:22-23).
input.aiCost: AI cost is NOT incurred in the progress/mastery write path at all — recordTestSubmitted, applyTrialKtpResults, markLessonComplete, getProgressCounts make zero LLM calls. AI cost appears in two adjacent places: (1) getPersonalSnapshot's optional insight layer (roadmap.service.ts:437-468) — gated on includeAiInsights && OPENAI_API_KEY — costs ~one LLM call PER personal-roadmap read PER user (coachSummary + per-node hints over last-20 test history); it is recomputed every request, NOT cached, so cost scales with reads, not nodes. getHomeOverview deliberately calls it with includeAiInsights:false to avoid this. (2) Test question generation and the lazy AI answer-explanation (testResult.service.buildAiExplanation → aiService.analyzeAnswers) — per attempt, generated lazily on first request to /ai-explanation and cached in testHistory.aiFeedback. Results are per-user (not shared); roadmap AI insights are per-user-per-subject-per-request and shared with nobody.
problems: Inconsistent mastery thresholds create confusing semantics: a regular node test is 'mastered' at bestScore>=70 (ROADMAP_MASTERY_THRESHOLD_PERCENT, roadmap.rules.ts:8) but the SAME test counts as a 'fail' (lowScoreFailCount++) unless scorePercent>=80 (ROADMAP_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_PASS_PERCENT, line 17). So a 70-79% score simultaneously marks the node mastered AND increments the fail counter (recordTestSubmitted lines 834-839). A user can be 'mastered' yet eventually blocked from retaking the test.

Whole-array rewrite on every progress mutation: recordTestSubmitted/markLessonComplete/markLessonRead/acknowledgeMaterialMastery all do filter+push then $set the ENTIRE nodes[] array (e.g. roadmap.service.ts:849-863). Not atomic at the element level and contends on the single document — concurrent submits for different nodes can lost-update each other (read-modify-write race) since there is no positional/arrayFilters update or optimistic version check.

recordTestSubmitted has a non-atomic gap between RoadmapAttempt.create (idempotency marker, line 805) and the progress write (line 853): if the process dies between them, the attempt is recorded as 'seen' but mastery is never applied, and the next retry short-circuits as idempotent (line 793) — silently dropping the mastery update.

mergeProgressWithCanonical is called 3+ times per snapshot (lines 299/309/320) and recordTestSubmitted/getPickerSubjects re-run a full getPersonalSnapshot (which itself re-reads + may re-upsert) — repeated normalize+merge work and extra DB round-trips per request.

N+1 / fan-out cost: getPersonalSnapshot does a per-node Test.findOne to resolve testId (enrichPersonalNodeView line 259) — one query per node; getHomeOverview and getPickerSubjects loop getPersonalSnapshot across all learner subjects, multiplying this. getPickerSubjects also re-queries UserRoadmapProgress AFTER getPersonalSnapshot already loaded it (line 702).

ROADMAP_BEST_SCORE_WINDOW_N (roadmap.rules.ts:23) is declared and documented as a rolling-window option but never used — bestScore is a permanent global max, so a node can never lose mastery even if recent performance collapses (no decay).

Mastery granularity is node-only. q.relatedContent.topicId carries finer signal and testResult.service builds per-topic 'topicsToReview', and trial fans topics out to KTP nodes, but none of this finer-grained correctness is persisted as sub-node mastery — it is recomputed/discarded each time.

canonicalVersion stored on the progress doc is a derived timestamp (Math.floor(max(updatedAt)/1000)); any touch of Subject.updatedAt (e.g. rebuildCanonicalFromKtp line 1069, or unrelated edits) bumps the version and forces a progress re-merge/re-save (line 310), causing write amplification on otherwise-unchanged progress.

applyTrialKtpResults and recordTestSubmitted duplicate near-identical merge→update node→reorder→upsert logic (lines 747-770 vs 828-863) — missing a shared 'mutateNodeProgress(nodeId, fn)' helper; the trial path also forgets to reset/handle lowScoreFailCount, diverging behavior.
evidence: NodeProgressSchema (UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts:12): `nodeId: {type:String, required:true}, mastered:{type:Boolean, required:true, default:false}, bestScore:{type:Number, required:true, default:0, min:0, max:100}, lowScoreFailCount:{type:Number, default:0, min:0}, lessons:{type:[LessonProgressSchema], default:undefined}, lessonReadAt:{type:Date}`

UNIQUE keys: UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts:36 `UserRoadmapProgressSchema.index({ userId:1, subjectId:1 }, { unique:true })`; RoadmapAttempt.model.ts:18 `RoadmapAttemptSchema.index({ userId:1, sessionId:1 }, { unique:true })`

Unlock logic (roadmap.service.ts:60): `function availabilityForNode(node, mastered){ if(node.prerequisites.length===0) return 'available'; const allDone = node.prerequisites.every((id)=>mastered.has(id)); return allDone ? 'available' : 'locked'; }`
masteredSet (roadmap.service.ts:48-58): builds Set of nodeIds where `p?.mastered` is true — the single input to availability and unlock counting. No persisted 'available'/'locked' field exists.

Mastery + fail-counter update on test completion (roadmap.service.ts:833-839): `const bestScore = Math.max(cur.bestScore, scorePercent); const mastered = cur.mastered || isMasteredByBestScore(bestScore); const prevFail = cur.lowScoreFailCount ?? 0; const lowScoreFailCount = scorePercent >= ROADMAP_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_PASS_PERCENT ? 0 : Math.min(ROADMAP_MAX_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_FAILS_BEFORE_BLOCK, prevFail + 1);`
Block enforcement (roadmap.service.ts:167): `if (count >= ROADMAP_MAX_KNOWLEDGE_TEST_FAILS_BEFORE_BLOCK) throw AppError.forbidden('По этой теме уже трижды не набрали 80%...')`

Idempotency short-circuit (roadmap.service.ts:793-802): `const existingAttempt = await RoadmapAttempt.findOne({ userId, sessionId }); if (existingAttempt) { ... return { idempotent:true, updatedNodesDelta:[], ... } }`

Trial mastery application (roadmap.service.ts:754-757): `const bestScore = Math.max(cur.bestScore, r.scorePercent); const mastered = cur.mastered || isMasteredByTrialScore(r.scorePercent) || isMasteredByBestScore(bestScore); const updated = { ...cur, nodeId:r.nodeId, bestScore, mastered };`

Trial row builder threshold (trialTopicMastery.util.ts:44-45): `const scorePercent = Math.round((v.correct / v.total) * 100); if (scorePercent < ROADMAP_TRIAL_MASTERY_THRESHOLD_PERCENT) continue;` then fans out via `mapBookTopicToKtpNodeIds`

Submit wiring (test.controller.ts:333-342): `if (roadmapNodeId?.trim() && roadmapSessionId?.trim()) { roadmap = await roadmapService.recordTestSubmitted({ userId, subjectId: test.subjectId.toString(), nodeId: roadmapNodeId.trim(), scorePercent: resultSummary.scorePercent, sessionId: roadmapSessionId.trim(), submittedAt: new Date() }); }`

Profile stats reuse of node counts (profileStats.service.ts:62): `const { nodesTotal, nodesMastered } = await roadmapService.getProgressCounts(userId, sid); total += nodesTotal; studied += nodesMastered;`

content.text:
Done. I mapped the PROGRESS / ATTEMPTS / MASTERY subsystem and returned it via StructuredOutput.

Key findings (all file:line evidence is in the structured payload):

- Per-user progress is stored as ONE `UserRoadmapProgress` document per `(userId, subjectId)` (unique index, `UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts:36`) with an EMBEDDED `nodes[]` array — not a row per node. Persisted per-node state is `mastered` (bool), `bestScore` (0..100 global max), `lowScoreFailCount`, plus a separate lesson-reading gate (`lessons[]`/`lessonReadAt`).
- There is NO persisted locked/available state. Availability is computed at read time in `availabilityForNode` (`roadmap.service.ts:60`): a node is `available` iff it has no prerequisites or every prerequisite nodeId is in the mastered set; otherwise `locked`.
- Completing a test (`POST /tests/submit` with `roadmapNodeId`+`roadmapSessionId`) calls `recordTestSubmitted` (`roadmap.service.ts:776`): it writes a `RoadmapAttempt` (idempotency keyed on unique `{userId, sessionId}`), sets `bestScore = max(prev, score)`, `mastered = mastered || bestScore>=70`, and `lowScoreFailCount = score>=80 ? 0 : min(3, prev+1)`. The next node "unlocks" implicitly because the recomputed mastered set flips its availability — no explicit unlock write.
- Mastery is tracked ONLY at node level (node = KTP topic). Finer signal (per-topic correctness in `testResult.service`, KTP fan-out in `trialTopicMastery.util`) is computed and discarded, never persisted as sub-node mastery.
- "Trial" (пробник) is an ENT-shaped full-exam mode (`trial.service.ts`): 3 fixed main blocks + 2 chosen profile subjects. It differs from the normal node-test flow in threshold (>=80 vs >=70), it fans book-topics out to KTP nodes (`applyTrialKtpResults`, `roadmap.service.ts:735`), and it does not touch `lowScoreFailCount`.

Notable issues called out: the 70-vs-80 threshold split means a 70-79% score marks a node mastered AND increments its fail counter; full-array rewrites on every mutation create a lost-update race; and a crash between `RoadmapAttempt.create` and the progress write can silently drop a mastery update due to the idempotency short-circuit.

Relevant files:
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/RoadmapAttempt.model.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/roadmap.service.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/roadmap/roadmap.rules.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/types/roadmap.types.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/controllers/test.controller.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/utils/trialTopicMastery.util.ts
- /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/trial.service.ts
